dashboard.title: Water sampling — ops view
dashboard.description: Plain-language view for field and ops staff. “What to sample / order” is the test the lab needs. Refresh after each CSV import.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*)::int AS value
FROM water_sampling_schedule
WHERE next_due_date IS NOT NULL
  AND next_due_date < CURRENT_DATE

SELECT COUNT(*)::int AS value
FROM water_sampling_schedule
WHERE next_due_date IS NOT NULL
  AND next_due_date >= CURRENT_DATE
  AND next_due_date <= (CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '45 days')::date

panels.title: Notes say DUE NOW / PAST DUE

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(*)::int AS value
FROM water_sampling_schedule
WHERE notes IS NOT NULL
  AND (
    UPPER(notes) LIKE '%DUE NOW%'
    OR UPPER(notes) LIKE '%PAST DUE%'
  )

panels.title: TESTS TO ORDER OR SCHEDULE — next 120 days
panels.description: Each row is one lab test to plan for. “What to sample / order” tells you the analyte or panel to order. Sort is soonest due date first.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(source_file) LIKE '%standby%' THEN 'Standby well'
    WHEN LOWER(source_file) LIKE '%active%' THEN 'Active well'
    ELSE 'Well (rename file to include active or standby)'
  END AS "Well",
  CASE
    WHEN next_due_date IS NULL THEN 'No due date in file'
    WHEN next_due_date < CURRENT_DATE THEN 'OVERDUE'
    WHEN next_due_date <= (CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '14 days')::date THEN 'Due within 2 weeks'
    WHEN next_due_date <= (CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '45 days')::date THEN 'Due within 45 days'
    WHEN next_due_date <= (CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '120 days')::date THEN 'Due within 120 days'
    ELSE 'OK'
  END AS "How soon",
  CASE
    WHEN COALESCE(TRIM(analyte_name), '') = '' AND COALESCE(TRIM(group_name), '') = '' THEN '(see PS code — analyte missing in export)'
    WHEN COALESCE(TRIM(group_name), '') = '' THEN TRIM(analyte_name)
    WHEN COALESCE(TRIM(analyte_name), '') = '' THEN TRIM(group_name)
    ELSE TRIM(group_name) || ' — ' || TRIM(analyte_name)
  END AS "What to sample / order",
  next_due_date AS "Due date",
  last_sampled AS "Last sampled",
  ps_code AS "PS code",
  COALESCE(notes, '') AS "Notes",
  source_file AS "Source file"
FROM water_sampling_schedule
WHERE next_due_date IS NOT NULL
  AND next_due_date <= (CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '120 days')::date
ORDER BY next_due_date ASC, "Well", ps_code

panels.title: Lead and copper — what to sample
panels.description: Lead/copper schedule from your two-column import. Order bottles for the analyte shown.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(source_file) LIKE '%standby%' THEN 'Standby well'
    WHEN LOWER(source_file) LIKE '%active%' THEN 'Active well'
    ELSE 'Well (rename file to include active or standby)'
  END AS "Well",
  TRIM(analyte_name) AS "What to sample / order",
  next_due_date AS "Due date",
  COALESCE(notes, '') AS "Notes",
  ps_code AS "PS code",
  source_file AS "Source file"
FROM water_sampling_schedule
WHERE group_name = 'LEAD_COPPER'
ORDER BY "Well", "What to sample / order"

panels.title: URGENT — notes say DUE NOW or PAST DUE
panels.description: These rows need a human check against DWW even if a date is missing.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(source_file) LIKE '%standby%' THEN 'Standby well'
    WHEN LOWER(source_file) LIKE '%active%' THEN 'Active well'
    ELSE 'Well (rename file to include active or standby)'
  END AS "Well",
  CASE
    WHEN COALESCE(TRIM(analyte_name), '') = '' AND COALESCE(TRIM(group_name), '') = '' THEN '(see PS code)'
    WHEN COALESCE(TRIM(group_name), '') = '' THEN TRIM(analyte_name)
    WHEN COALESCE(TRIM(analyte_name), '') = '' THEN TRIM(group_name)
    ELSE TRIM(group_name) || ' — ' || TRIM(analyte_name)
  END AS "What to sample / order",
  COALESCE(notes, '') AS "Notes",
  next_due_date AS "Due date",
  ps_code AS "PS code",
  source_file AS "Source file"
FROM water_sampling_schedule
WHERE notes IS NOT NULL
  AND (
    UPPER(notes) LIKE '%DUE NOW%'
    OR UPPER(notes) LIKE '%PAST DUE%'
  )
ORDER BY "Well", ps_code

panels.title: Files loaded (import status)
panels.description: One line per CSV file name you imported. Re-import replaces that file’s rows.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  source_file AS "File name",
  COUNT(*)::bigint AS "Rows",
  MAX(imported_at) AS "Last import"
FROM water_sampling_schedule
GROUP BY source_file
ORDER BY source_file

panels.title: Full list — all tests (reference)
panels.description: Complete schedule for lookup. Use the panels above for day-to-day work.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN LOWER(source_file) LIKE '%standby%' THEN 'Standby well'
    WHEN LOWER(source_file) LIKE '%active%' THEN 'Active well'
    ELSE 'Well (rename file)'
  END AS "Well",
  CASE
    WHEN COALESCE(TRIM(analyte_name), '') = '' AND COALESCE(TRIM(group_name), '') = '' THEN '(unspecified)'
    WHEN COALESCE(TRIM(group_name), '') = '' THEN TRIM(analyte_name)
    WHEN COALESCE(TRIM(analyte_name), '') = '' THEN TRIM(group_name)
    ELSE TRIM(group_name) || ' — ' || TRIM(analyte_name)
  END AS "What to sample / order",
  next_due_date AS "Due date",
  last_sampled AS "Last sampled",
  ps_code AS "PS code",
  COALESCE(group_name, '') AS "Group",
  source_file AS "Source file"
FROM water_sampling_schedule
ORDER BY "Well", next_due_date NULLS LAST, ps_code

message: Automated upsert from neon-loader (water compliance ops view)
